agency_name: National Health and Medical Research Council (NHMRC)
executive_summary: NHMRC is an independent statutory authority and Australia's primary health-and-medical-research funder, operating as a hybrid research-funder/regulator/guidelines body. This year its deliberate agenda is dominated by enabling and reform work: supporting development and 2026 implementation of Australia's first National Health and Medical Research Strategy, harmonising the MRFF and MREA grant programs under shared advisory structures, refreshing the Sapphire grants management platform, stabilising financial sustainability, reforming peer review, and strengthening research integrity. External pressure is real and current — record-low Ideas Grant success rates (~8%) and sector criticism of peer-review fairness make process redesign, evaluation and assurance politically live. The cleanest BD fits are Digital & ICT Delivery (Sapphire/ICT/data), Financial & Accounting (financial-sustainability uplift), Strategy, Policy & Governance (peer-review/evaluation/strategy), and Audit & Assurance (grants integrity), though NHMRC's APS Strategic Commissioning Framework target to insource core ICT roles tempers some technology demand.

strategic_priorities.title: Support the National Health and Medical Research Strategy
strategic_priorities.description: Work with the Department of Health, Disability and Ageing to finalise and release Australia's first National Health and Medical Research Strategy, with public consultation across H2 2025 and implementation from 2026.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Finalise and release the National Strategy within the next 12 months commencing with consultation on the draft National Strategy in the second half of 2025.

strategic_priorities.title: Harmonise the MRFF and MREA
strategic_priorities.description: Optimise synergies and align policy, operations and governance between the MRFF (administered with the Department) and NHMRC's MREA grant program, using the shared NHMRC-MRFF advisory committee structure in place since 1 July 2024.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Continue to use the shared advisory structure through Council/AMRAB and expert advisory committees to create alignment in policy and operations.

strategic_priorities.title: Maintain excellence in peer review
strategic_priorities.description: Innovate, evaluate and enhance how researchers are assessed for grants — peer reviewer training, expanding consumer roles, refining score descriptors, feedback to applicants and reducing reviewer burden.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Continue to implement actions aligned with NHMRC's framework for peer review and responsible research assessment.

strategic_priorities.title: Bolster confidence in research integrity
strategic_priorities.description: With the ARC, progress potential reform of the national research integrity framework and update NHMRC policies for managing misconduct, fraud and corruption, including releasing the Good Institutional Practice Guide.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Progress discussion about potential reform of the national research integrity framework, as well as updating NHMRC-specific policies and processes.

strategic_priorities.title: Refresh the Sapphire grants platform
strategic_priorities.description: Embark on a refresh of NHMRC's end-to-end grant management system, shifting how Sapphire is hosted, supported and developed to build a centralised, maintainable, resilient platform that reduces administrative burden and optimises data capability.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: This year will see us embark on our refresh project for NHMRC's end-to-end grant management system, Sapphire. This project marks a shift in how the platform will be hosted, supported and developed.

strategic_priorities.title: Stabilise financial sustainability
strategic_priorities.description: Mature oversight and assurance over the annual operating budget and project investment by embedding a robust multi-year internal budget and stronger financial governance and compliance frameworks.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Mature NHMRC's approach to oversight and assurance over the agency's annual operating budget and project investment, including embedding the robust multi-year internal budget.

strategic_priorities.title: Optimise ICT and data capability
strategic_priorities.description: Mature core ICT and data capabilities through service-delivery, ICT governance, project delivery and data/information management practices, including launching an ICT Strategy and finalising a Data and Information Strategy.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Improve core ICT and data capabilities through ongoing maturation of service delivery, ICT governance, project delivery, and data/information management practices.

strategic_priorities.title: Foster translation and commercialisation
strategic_priorities.description: Refresh NHMRC's Research Translation Strategy for 2026 and beyond and expand industry engagement (biotech and health-connected companies) to drive commercialisation and local production of treatments and devices.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Work with advisory committees and stakeholders to review and refresh NHMRC's Research Translation Strategy for 2026 and beyond.

reform_and_change_drivers: First-ever National Health and Medical Research Strategy being finalised in 2025 for implementation from 2026, setting a unified national direction and reshaping coordination of the research ecosystem
Harmonisation of MRFF and MREA grant programs under shared NHMRC-MRFF advisory committees (in effect since 1 July 2024)
Sapphire grants platform refresh — a shift in hosting, support and development model
APS Strategic Commissioning Framework target to reduce 2 core ICT contractor roles in 2025-26 and transition enabling ICT from outsourced to a hybrid in-house/managed-service model
Exploration of AI and large language models for data insights, research impact reporting and evaluation
Rising cybersecurity risk and increasing demands around data access, sharing, transparency and open science
Government reform initiatives on integrity, fraud and corruption control and APS capability
Sector pressure from record-low grant success rates and contested peer-review fairness
challenges_and_risks: Financial sustainability of operations and project investment — named capability priority and an enterprise risk on fiscal control
Sapphire/ICT solutions operating suboptimally or increasing stakeholder burden (rated Moderate tolerance — the highest in the risk table)
Cyber and information-security threats to ICT platforms and data
Grant application/review processes being an unreasonable burden on researchers and peer reviewers
Research misconduct, fraud or corruption in funded research not adequately managed; foreign interference in NHMRC-funded research
Workforce capability and retention — reliance on a suitably skilled workforce while insourcing core work under the Commissioning Framework
Maintaining community trust in science amid misinformation and contested funding fairness
Dependence on third-party providers for business outcomes

news_and_controversies.summary: NHMRC's Ideas Grants success rate fell to ~8.1% (190 of 2,347 funded), down from 10.1% the prior year; peak bodies (AAMRI, ASMR) publicly criticised the system's impact on careers and researcher wellbeing while NHMRC acknowledged concern over low funded rates despite a record ~A$280m round.
news_and_controversies.implication: A live, politically sensitive pressure point — NHMRC must defend the integrity and efficiency of its allocation model, opening advisory work on process redesign, sector-impact analysis, evaluation and stakeholder engagement.
news_and_controversies.source: Research Professional News, 'Success rate for NHMRC Ideas Grants dips below 10 per cent' — https://www.researchprofessionalnews.com/rr-news-australia-government-agencies-2026-1-success-rate-for-nhmrc-ideas-grants-dips-below-10-per-cent/

news_and_controversies.summary: Academic analysis argued NHMRC's 'application-centric' peer-review model (≈5 independent reviewers, no deliberating panels) is vulnerable to assessor bias, conflicts of interest and statistical noise, and that real per-capita funding had fallen materially over a decade.
news_and_controversies.implication: Recognised methodological weaknesses in scoring and peer review create scope for evaluation-design, data-analytics and assurance support — NHMRC was already commissioning peer-review reform advice.
news_and_controversies.source: The Conversation, 'More money and smarter choices: how to fix Australia's broken NHMRC medical research funding system' (Aug 2022) — https://theconversation.com/more-money-and-smarter-choices-how-to-fix-australias-broken-nhmrc-medical-research-funding-system-188003

news_and_controversies.summary: Long-running criticism that the assessment process disadvantages younger and female researchers and rewards safe, established projects; NHMRC has acknowledged shortcomings and undertaken structural review of the grant program.
news_and_controversies.implication: Equity, diversity and program-design reform is a recurring theme aligning with NHMRC's 'Embed equity' and 'Maintain excellence in peer review' priorities — relevant for policy evaluation and equity-impact analysis.
news_and_controversies.source: The Conversation, 'Is the NHMRC funding process fair?' — https://theconversation.com/is-the-nhmrc-funding-process-fair-67778

anao_findings.summary: ANAO performance audit 'Administration of Grants by the NHMRC' found shortcomings significantly impacting effectiveness — inconsistent application of guidelines including conflict-of-interest provisions, poor post-award compliance and acquittal backlogs, unsystematic debt recovery, and grants-management systems that did not adequately support administration or outcome reporting (data ~2009; dated).
anao_findings.implication: Conflict-of-interest controls, grants acquittals/assurance and grants-management-system capability are documented historical weak points — durable, credible themes for assurance and Digital/ICT work, though years of remediation may have occurred.
anao_findings.source: ANAO, 'Administration of Grants by the National Health and Medical Research Council' — https://www.anao.gov.au/work/performance-audit/administration-grants-the-national-health-and-medical-research-council

anao_findings.summary: An ANAO performance audit on NHMRC's fraud-control arrangements exists; publication date and detailed findings were not confirmed in the research.
anao_findings.implication: Indicates fraud control/integrity has been an audit focus — a potential governance/assurance line; currency should be verified before relying on it.
anao_findings.source: ANAO, 'Fraud Control Arrangements in the NHMRC' — https://www.anao.gov.au/work/performance-audit/fraud-control-arrangements-the-national-health-and-medical-research-council

anao_findings.summary: NHMRC was a designated entity in Auditor-General Report No.46 of 2019-20 (25 June 2020) examining whether Education and Health portfolio entities had appropriately implemented agreed ANAO and parliamentary-committee recommendations.
anao_findings.implication: Shows NHMRC is in scope for ANAO follow-up on recommendation implementation — relevant to audit-remediation tracking and governance uplift (note: 2020, just outside the 3-year window).
anao_findings.source: ANAO, 'Implementation of ANAO and Parliamentary Committee Recommendations — Education and Health Portfolios' — https://www.anao.gov.au/work/performance-audit/implementation-anao-parliamentary-committee-recommendations-education-health-portfolios

parliamentary_matters.summary: NHMRC sits within the Health portfolio scrutinised by the Senate Community Affairs Legislation Committee at Estimates, the standard forum for questions on notice and Hansard exchanges on NHMRC funding and performance. No specific recent inquiry, Estimates exchange or QoN naming NHMRC was confirmed (searches were truncated).
parliamentary_matters.implication: Estimates is the most likely venue for emerging scrutiny of grant success rates and funding adequacy; monitor Community Affairs Legislation Committee transcripts and QoN for signals that convert media pressure into formal demand.
parliamentary_matters.source: Parliament of Australia, Senate Estimates (Community Affairs Legislation Committee) — https://www.aph.gov.au/Parliamentary_Business/Senate_Estimates/clacctte/estimates/index

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Re-platforming Sapphire with a new hosting/support/development model to build a centralised, maintainable, resilient grants system that reduces administrative burden and optimises data — the highest-tolerance enterprise risk (Moderate) in the plan.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Discovery and solution design for the Sapphire re-platform, with business/systems/process analysis, build/integration support and delivery of a hybrid hosting/managed-service operating model; right-sized around NHMRC's intent to lift in-house capability.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan explicitly names a Sapphire refresh as a capability priority and a strategic priority, flags a shift in hosting/support, and rates ICT-solution risk Moderate (highest in the table). Note the APS Commissioning Framework insourcing target means positioning as managed-service-where-more-efficient, not wholesale outsourcing.

opportunity_areas.area: Financial sustainability and budget governance uplift
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Stabilise financial sustainability by embedding a robust multi-year internal budget and stronger financial governance and compliance frameworks, with oversight and assurance over operating budget and project investment.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A financial-sustainability review plus design of a multi-year internal budgeting model and strengthened financial governance/compliance framework, with embedding support for the Portfolio Investment Committee and Executive Board.
opportunity_areas.rationale: 'Stabilise financial sustainability' is a named capability priority and maps directly to an enterprise risk on poor fiscal control; the plan calls for embedding a multi-year budget and stronger financial governance.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Maintain excellence in peer review and reduce reviewer burden while defending fairness, equity and efficiency of the allocation model amid record-low success rates and sector criticism.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A peer-review/responsible-research-assessment redesign engagement — options analysis on assessment models, score-descriptor refinement, reviewer-burden reduction and a sector-impact assessment — with Data, Analytics & Information as secondary for the supporting analytics.
opportunity_areas.rationale: 'Maintain excellence in peer review' is a strategic priority with specific 2025-26 actions, and external coverage (record-low Ideas Grant success rate, contested application-centric model) makes this a live, defensible reform need.

opportunity_areas.area: National Strategy implementation and MRFF/MREA harmonisation
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Translate the first National Health and Medical Research Strategy into implementation from 2026 and continue aligning MRFF and MREA policy, operations and governance.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Implementation planning and PMO/delivery support for the National Strategy roll-out and the MRFF-MREA harmonisation program, including a benefits-management framework and change support across the shared committee structure.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Two strategic priorities — Support the National Strategy and Harmonise the MRFF and MREA — name multi-year, cross-agency change commencing implementation in 2026, the classic shape of program/change demand.

opportunity_areas.area: Research integrity framework reform and grants assurance
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Reform the national research integrity framework with the ARC, update misconduct/fraud/corruption policies, and address historical grants-administration and conflict-of-interest weaknesses.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: An assurance/health-check of NHMRC's research-integrity and grants-administration controls (including conflict-of-interest management and acquittal/compliance processes), supporting the Good Institutional Practice Guide rollout and Audit and Risk Committee oversight.
opportunity_areas.rationale: 'Bolster confidence in research integrity' is a strategic priority; enterprise risks name misconduct/fraud/corruption (Very Low tolerance); and ANAO history flags conflict-of-interest and grants-compliance weaknesses — together a credible assurance case.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Finalise and launch an ICT Strategy and a Data and Information Strategy and mature ICT governance, project delivery and data/information management.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: An information/data strategy and maturity uplift — data governance and management framework, analytics roadmap and business-intelligence design to underpin grant data and impact reporting.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan commits to finalising a Data and Information Strategy and optimising ICT/data capability as a named priority, with data-sharing and open-science trends in the environment scan.

opportunity_areas.area: AI/LLM for research impact reporting and evaluation
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Explore AI and large language models to provide new insights into NHMRC data and support research impact reporting and evaluation (incl. a quantitative impact measure using AI-enabled approaches from 2026-27).
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A scoped analytics/AI pilot to support impact reporting and evaluation — bibliometric/economic/LLM-enabled impact analysis linked to external datasets — governed by NHMRC's generative-AI policy.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The CEO message and performance measure 10 explicitly commit to exploring AI/LLMs for data insights and impact reporting, with an AI-enabled performance measure scheduled for 2026-27.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Deliver the Cyber Security Strategy and maintain security controls amid increasing cyber threats to ICT platforms and grant-scheme data integrity.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A security assessment and assurance of core ICT systems (including Sapphire) and the data they hold, plus advisory support to deliver Cyber Security Strategy priorities and annual reviews.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Cybersecurity is named in capability/safety-and-security sections, with two enterprise risks on malicious cyber activity and inappropriate technology use; cyber risk is also called out in the environment scan.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Continuous evaluation and improvement of grant program policies and outcomes, guided by the NHMRC Evaluation Strategy, with several newly-revised performance measures.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Program evaluation services and a PGPA-aligned performance-measure review supporting the Evaluation Strategy, including process/outcome evaluation of grant-program reforms.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan commits to continuous evaluation and references an Evaluation Strategy; several performance targets were revised for clarity, signalling an evolving measurement framework.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Develop a workforce strategy for a capable, adaptable, future-ready workforce while reducing outsourcing of core work under the APS Strategic Commissioning Framework.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Workforce planning and capability-uplift support to underpin the insourcing transition (especially core ICT), plus organisational/learning-and-development design — positioned as building NHMRC's own capability, not contracting it out.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan commits to developing a workforce strategy and a Learning and Development Strategy and names an enterprise risk on maintaining a skilled workforce; but the Commissioning Framework's anti-outsourcing intent limits fit.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Enhance external communication and outreach to involve consumers/stakeholders, sustain community trust in medical science and counter misinformation.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Stakeholder-engagement and communications support for consultations (e.g. National Strategy roundtables, guidelines public consultation) and trust-building campaigns.
opportunity_areas.rationale: 'Enhance external communication and outreach' is a named capability priority, and the plan emphasises consultation-heavy activity (National Strategy, guidelines, consumer involvement); however much of this is core public-engagement work NHMRC may keep in-house.

recommended_actions.action: Approach the Sapphire/grants technology owners (Program Management Committee, the grants/ICT division and the head of ICT) with a Sapphire re-platform discovery and managed-service delivery proposition framed around NHMRC's hybrid in-house/managed-service intent.
recommended_actions.rationale: The Sapphire refresh is the single clearest, time-bound, budgeted technology opportunity (named priority + Moderate-tolerance risk), and the plan signals a deliberate shift in hosting/support/development model this year.

recommended_actions.action: Offer the CFO/finance function a short financial-sustainability review and multi-year internal-budget/financial-governance design, referencing the named 'Stabilise financial sustainability' priority and the Audit and Risk Committee's assurance role.
recommended_actions.rationale: A discrete, low-political-risk enabling priority that maps cleanly to Financial & Accounting and is explicitly flagged for embedding this year.

recommended_actions.action: Develop a thought piece on defensible, lower-burden peer-review/allocation models and equity-impact analysis, and seek a meeting with the branch owning peer review and responsible research assessment.
recommended_actions.rationale: Record-low success rates and public criticism make peer-review reform a live, reputationally sensitive issue that aligns directly with a stated strategic priority — a strong door-opener.

recommended_actions.action: Prepare a grants-integrity/assurance capability statement (conflict-of-interest controls, acquittals/compliance, fraud control) for the Audit and Risk Committee and integrity function, referencing the integrity-reform priority and ANAO history.
recommended_actions.rationale: Integrity reform is a strategic priority and ANAO has historically flagged COI and grants-compliance weaknesses; assurance work here is credible and recurring.

recommended_actions.action: Watch Senate Community Affairs Legislation Committee Estimates and QoN for emerging scrutiny of funding adequacy/success rates, and track release of the National Strategy (from 2026) for downstream implementation procurements.
recommended_actions.rationale: Parliamentary pressure could convert current media criticism into formal demand, and National Strategy implementation will generate multi-year program/change opportunities.

engagement_targets: Chief Executive Officer / Executive Board (accountable authority for strategy and reform agenda)
Program Management Committee and the grants/ICT division (Sapphire refresh, grant systems)
Chief Information Officer / head of ICT (ICT Strategy, Data and Information Strategy, cyber, insourcing transition)
Chief Financial Officer / finance function and Portfolio Investment Committee (financial sustainability, multi-year budget)
Audit and Risk Committee and the research-integrity/fraud-control function (integrity reform, assurance)
Branch owning peer review and responsible research assessment (peer-review reform)
National Strategy / MRFF-MREA harmonisation program area (within NHMRC and jointly with the Department of Health, Disability and Ageing)
talking_points: You're re-platforming Sapphire and shifting its hosting/support model — we can run discovery and build/integration and stand up a hybrid managed-service that complements your in-house ICT uplift, not replaces it.

Financial sustainability is a named priority and a fiscal-control risk — we can deliver a focused review and design your multi-year internal budget and financial-governance framework for the Audit and Risk Committee.

With Ideas Grant success rates near 8% and public scrutiny of peer-review fairness, we can help redesign a more defensible, lower-burden assessment model backed by analytics and equity-impact evidence.

ANAO has historically flagged conflict-of-interest and grants-compliance gaps — as you reform the integrity framework and roll out the Good Institutional Practice Guide, we can provide independent assurance over your grants and integrity controls.
You're exploring AI/LLMs for impact reporting and have an AI-enabled performance measure from 2026-27 — we can scope a governed pilot linking your grant data to external impact datasets.
As the National Strategy moves to implementation from 2026, we can provide PMO/delivery and benefits-management support across the shared NHMRC-MRFF structure.
caveats: The plan is strategy-and-priority rich but light on dollar figures, procurement timing and program scale, so opportunity sizing is inferred. NHMRC is a small, expertise-heavy funder/regulator — engagements should be right-sized (short advisory and framework/design pieces, plus a genuinely scalable Sapphire build), not large transactional delivery. A material constraint is NHMRC's explicit APS Strategic Commissioning Framework target to reduce core ICT contractors and insource enabling ICT, which narrows pure ICT-advisory demand and means technology offers must be positioned as managed-service-where-more-efficient or capability-building. External research found pressure on funding adequacy and peer-review fairness rather than governance scandal, and ANAO findings cited are dated; parliamentary coverage was under-explored due to truncated searches and should be re-run before finalising.
